query: EU AI Act AI Omnibus watermarking obligation Art. 50 AI synthetic content postpone grandfathering deadline December 2026

{"query":"EU AI Act AI Omnibus watermarking obligation Art. 50 AI synthetic content postpone grandfathering deadline December 2026","model":"mako","response_time_ms":2041,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.gibsondunn.com/eu-ai-act-omnibus-agreement-postponed-high-risk-deadlines-and-other-key-changes/","title":"EU AI Act Omnibus Agreement — Postponed High-Risk Deadlines and Other Key Changes - Gibson Dunn","content":"Businesses who are subject to those obligations must stay ready for that date regardless of the Omnibus.
Under the proposal, AI systems that have been placed on the market before 2 August 2026 will benefit from a four-month grace period (until 2 December 2026) before the watermarking obligation under Article 50(2) — which requires providers to embed machine-readable markers in AI-generated content — applies to them.
2 Aug 2027 — Deadline for member states to establish AI regulatory sandboxes (Article 57). — New — Annex I HRAIS obligations were originally due from this date but are proposed to be deferred to 2 Aug 2028.
The 2027/2028 dates for high-risk AI systems provide real headroom, but a compliance framework takes time to build properly."},{"url":"https://www.lw.com/en/insights/ai-act-update-eu-resolves-to-change-rules-and-extend-deadlines","title":"AI Act Update: EU Resolves to Change Rules and Extend Deadlines","content":"The Agreement clarifies existing AI Act requirements, extends compliance deadlines for high-risk AI systems (HRAIS), and introduces new rules on AI-generated intimate content.
The Agreement does not change the scope of existing AI Act transparency obligations under Art.
The text will proceed to formal adoption by the European Parliament and the Council, which is expected by July 2026 — ahead of August 2, 2026, when HRAIS requirements would otherwise take effect."},{"url":"https://www.resemble.ai/resources/the-eu-ai-act-what-generative-ai-companies-need-to-know-in-2026","title":"The EU AI Act: What Generative AI Companies Need to Know in 2026","content":"Key Takeaway: Article 50 requires both watermarking at creation (for AI providers) and deepfake detection and disclosure (for deployers).
Article 50 of the EU AI Act establishes clear transparency obligations for AI systems that generate synthetic content across all modalities.
Waiting until June or July 2026 leaves insufficient time for testing, documentation, and potential vendor procurement."},{"url":"https://www.aiactblog.nl/en/posts/article-50-transparency-deadline-2-august-2026","title":"Article 50 transparency obligations: the AI Act deadline of 2 August 2026 that has not been postponed","content":"Jun 13, 2026 — The Digital Omnibus political agreement of 7 May 2026 postponed the high-risk AI obligations to 2 December 2027 and 2 August 2028,
Here the focus is narrower and more urgent: what the Omnibus did and did not change, and why 2 August 2026 is still a live date.
Status on 13 June 2026: The Digital Omnibus reached a provisional political agreement between the Council and the European Parliament on 7 May 2026.
The agreement postpones the high-risk obligations under Annex III to 2 December 2027 and under Annex I to 2 August 2028.
Start by mapping every point where your organisation touches the four Article 50 situations: customer-facing chatbots and assistants, any generative model that produces public content, any deepfake or synthetic media production, and any AI-assisted text published to inform the public.
Article 50 is the next hard edge on the calendar, arriving on 2 August 2026, ahead of the postponed high-risk regime in late 2027.

tavily.raw: {"query":"EU AI Act AI Omnibus watermarking obligation Art. 50 AI synthetic content postpone grandfathering deadline December 2026","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.winstontaylor.com/insights/ai-act-rules-on-high-risk-ai-delayed-as-ai-digital-omnibus-agreed","title":"AI Act rules on high-risk AI delayed as AI Digital Omnibus agreed - Winston Taylor","content":"## AI Act rules on high-risk AI delayed as AI Digital Omnibus agreed. The EU has been under time pressure to agree the AI element of the Omnibus as its flagship provision is a postponement of the AI Act rules on high-risk AI which were due to apply from 2 August 2026. * **High-risk rules delayed:**the application of Chapter III AI Act on high-risk AI was originally linked to completion of standards and guidance. The final position is clearer – application dates are no longer linked to standards and will apply from **2 December 2027** (Annex III) and **2 August 2026** (Annex I). * **Watermarking requirements delayed:** transparency requirements on watermarking AI content (Article 50(2)) will now apply from **2 December 2026** instead of 2 August 2026 (a four month extension) for systems already on the market at 2 August 2026.","score":0.82795376,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://trufo.ai/resources/eu-ai-act","title":"EU AI Act Compliance for AI-Generated Content - Trufo","content":"The general deadline for Article 50 compliance is 2 August 2026. For ... Omnibus Resolution grants a grace period through 2 December 2026.","score":0.8116913,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://artificialintelligenceact.eu/transparency-rules-article-50","title":"The EU AI Act's Transparency Rules: A Practical Guide to ...","content":"# The EU AI Act’s Transparency Rules: A Practical Guide to Article 50. It introduces transparency obligations on providers and deployers of certain AI systems, under which users must be informed when they are interacting with an AI system or where content is AI-generated. While these obligations apply from 2 August 2026, the AI Omnibus provisional agreement of May 2026 grants generative AI systems already on the market before that date until 2 December 2026 to meet the machine-readable marking requirement under Article 50(2). Transparency obligations are not limited to systems classified as “high-risk”: they apply to any AI system used in the four situations the Article covers. Article 50 of the EU AI Act introduces transparency obligations in four situations:. The Commission has published draft Guidelines on the scope and application of Article 50, and a Code of Practice on AI-generated content is being developed to provide practical solutions on marking and labelling.","score":0.75027883,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.resemble.ai/resources/the-eu-ai-act-what-generative-ai-companies-need-to-know-in-2026","title":"What Generative AI Companies Need to Know in 2026","content":"# The EU AI Act: What Generative AI Companies Need to Know in 2026. >            \"The EU AI Act isn't just European regulation—it's setting the global standard for responsible AI development. For companies working with generative AI (whether that's voice cloning, text-to-speech, image generation, video synthesis, or multimodal systems) understanding Article 50 compliance isn't optional. **Key Takeaway:** Article 50 requires both watermarking at creation (for AI providers) and deepfake detection and disclosure (for deployers). With the August 2, 2026 enforcement deadline just months away and the final Code of Practice expected in June 2026, the time to act is now. ## What Is the EU AI Act? The EU AI Act (officially *Regulation (EU) 2024/1689*) is a risk-based regulatory framework that categorizes AI systems according to their potential harm to individuals and society. Article 50 of the EU AI Act establishes clear transparency obligations for AI systems that generate synthetic content across all modalities.","score":0.6551821,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.lw.com/en/insights/ai-act-update-eu-resolves-to-change-rules-and-extend-deadlines","title":"AI Act Update: EU Resolves to Change Rules and Extend ...","content":"# AI Act Update: EU Resolves to Change Rules and Extend Deadlines. EU lawmakers have agreed to reduce overlap of rules, introduce new prohibitions, and extend deadlines for high-risk AI systems. On May 7, 2026, EU legislative bodies reached a political agreement on proposed amendments to the AI Act (the Agreement). The Agreement clarifies existing AI Act requirements, extends compliance deadlines for high-risk AI systems (HRAIS), and introduces new rules on AI-generated intimate content. The European Commission (Commission) has also published guidelines and a draft Code of Practice addressing the AI Act’s transparency requirements. * extended deadlines for HRAIS obligations and watermarking transparency requirements;. The extension of the HRAIS compliance deadlines is a significant change in practice. The Commission faced calls to extend the compliance deadlines for HRAIS as it has not yet issued relevant harmonized standards and guidance to enable practical implementation within the original deadlines set out in the AI Act. The Agreement extends the compliance deadlines for stand-alone HRAIS to **December 2, 2027**.","score":0.6506676,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.74,"request_id":"9c4a6b90-ac7b-41cc-b032-c37d307de4bc"}
